content: In the circuit, the present value of $Z$ is $1$. Neglecting the delay in the combinatorial circuit, the values of $S$ and $Z$, respectively, after the application of the clock will be
A MOD-2 and a MOD-5 up-counter when cascaded together results in a MOD _________ counter. (in integer).
explanation:
For overall configuration = Mod (2 $$\times$$ 5) = 10

It will be mod 10 counter.

content: The maximum clock frequency in MHz of a 4-stage ripple counter, utilizing flip-flops, with each flip-flop having a propagation delay of 20 ns, is ________. (round off to one decimal place).
explanation:
Given ripple counter 4 flip-flop, tp = 20 nsec for each flip-flop.

T = ntp = 4 $$\times$$ 20 nsec = 80 nsec

Clock frequency $$ = {1 \over T} = {1 \over {4 \times 20 \times {{10}^{ - 9}}}} = 12.5$$ MHz

content: A 16 -bit synchronous binary up-counter is clocked with a frequency $f_{c l k}$. The two most significant bits are ORed together to form an output $Y$. Measurements shows that $Y$ is periodic and the duration for which $Y$ the remains high in each period is 24 ms . The clock frequency $f_{\text {clk }}$ is $\_\_\_\_$ MHz. (Round off to 2 decimal places)
explanation:
$$ \text { Given counter }=16 \text { bit } $$

$$ \begin{aligned} &2^{16} \text { combination }\left\{\begin{array}{lccrr} Q_{15} & Q_{14} & Q_{13} & Q_1 & Q_0 \\ 0 & 0 & 0 & \ldots \ldots \ldots 0 & 0 \\ 1 & 1 & 1 & \ldots \ldots \ldots .1 & 1 \end{array}\right.\\ &\text { If we fix } Q_{15} Q_{14} \text { bit then }\left(Q_{13} \text { to } Q_0\right) \text { will take } 2^{14} \text { combination } \end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|} \hline \boldsymbol{Y}=\boldsymbol{Q}_{15}+\boldsymbol{Q}_{14} & \boldsymbol{Q}_{15} & \boldsymbol{Q}_{14} & \boldsymbol{Q}_{13} \ldots \ldots \ldots \boldsymbol{Q}_1 \boldsymbol{Q}_0 \\ \hline 0\{ & \begin{array}{c} 0 \\ 0 \end{array} & \begin{array}{c} 0 \\ 0 \end{array} & \left\{2^{14}\right. \text { combination } \\ \hline 1\{ & \begin{array}{c} 0 \\ 0 \end{array} & \begin{array}{c} 1 \\ 1 \end{array} & \left\{2^{14}\right. \text { combination } \\ \hline 1\{ & 1 & 0 & \left\{2^{14}\right. \text { combination } \\ \hline 1\{ & 1 & 0 & 1 \\ & 1 & 1 & \left\{2^{14}\right. \text { combination } \\ \hline \end{array} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} &\text { }\\ &\begin{aligned} So,\,\, & Y=0 \text { for } 2^{14} \text { times. }[25 \%] \\ & Y=1 \text { for } 3 \times 2^{14} \text { times. }[75 \%] \end{aligned} \end{aligned} $$

Given that, $\quad Y=1$ for 24 ms

So, $Y=0$ for $\frac{24 \times 25}{75}=8 \mathrm{~ms}$

So, total time period $=8+24=32 \mathrm{~ms}$

Time period of clock, $T_{c l k}=\frac{32 \mathrm{~ms}}{2^{16}}$

$$ \begin{aligned} f_{c l k} & =\frac{2^{16}}{32 \times 10^{-3}} \\ f_{c l k} & =2.048 \mathrm{MHz} \approx 2.05 \mathrm{MHz} \end{aligned} $$

Hence, the correct answer is 2.05 .

content: For the ring counter shown in Fig. find the steady state sequence if the initial state of the counters is $$1110\,\,(i.e.,\,\,{Q_3},{Q_2},{Q_1},{Q_0} = 1110).$$ Determine the MOD number of the counter.

$$(a)$$ Construct the truth table for the circuit given in Figure $${Q_1},{Q_2}$$ and $${Q_3}$$ are outputs and the clock pulses are the inputs. Unused $$JK$$ inputs are assumed to be at logic $$1.$$ All flip flops are reset at power $$ON$$.
$$(b)$$ Sketch the output waveforms at $${Q_1},{Q_2}$$ and $${Q_3}$$.
$$(c)$$ What function does this circuit perform.
answer: $$(c)$$ This is MOD$$-5$$ hybrid counter here $${Q_2}$$ toggles when $${Q_1}$$ changes from $$1$$ to $$0.$$
